NKJV.1: I beseech you therefore, brethren, by the mercies of God, that you present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable to God, which is your reasonable service.
NKJV.2: And do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, that you may prove what is that good and acceptable and perfect will of God.
NKJV.3: For I say, through the grace given to me, to everyone who is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think, but to think soberly, as God has dealt to each one a measure of faith.
NKJV.4: For as we have many members in one body, but all the members do not have the same function,
NKJV.5: so we, being many, are one body in Christ, and individually members of one another.
NKJV.6: Having then gifts differing according to the grace that is given to us, let us use them: if prophecy, let us prophesy in proportion to our faith;
NKJV.7: or ministry, let us use it in our ministering; he who teaches, in teaching;
NKJV.8: he who exhorts, in exhortation; he who gives, with liberality; he who leads, with diligence; he who shows mercy, with cheerfulness.
NKJV.9: Let love be without hypocrisy. Abhor what is evil. Cling to what is good.
NKJV.10: Be kindly affectionate to one another with brotherly love, in honor giving preference to one another;
NKJV.11: not lagging in diligence, fervent in spirit, serving the Lord;
NKJV.12: rejoicing in hope, patient in tribulation, continuing steadfastly in prayer;
NKJV.13: distributing to the needs of the saints, given to hospitality.
NKJV.14: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse.
NKJV.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice, and weep with those who weep.
NKJV.16: Be of the same mind toward one another. Do not set your mind on high things, but associate with the humble. Do not be wise in your own opinion.
NKJV.17: Repay no one evil for evil. Have regard for good things in the sight of all men.
NKJV.18: If it is possible, as much as depends on you, live peaceably with all men.
NKJV.19: Beloved, do not avenge yourselves, but rather give place to wrath; for it is written, “Vengeance is Mine, I will repay,” says the Lord.
NKJV.20: Therefore  “If your enemy is hungry, feed him; If he is thirsty, give him a drink; For in so doing you will heap coals of fire on his head.”
NKJV.21: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

ESV.1: I appeal to you therefore, brothers,  by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your spiritual worship.
ESV.2: Do not be conformed to this world,  but be transformed by the renewal of your mind, that by testing you may discern what is the will of God, what is good and acceptable and perfect.
ESV.3: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think, but to think with sober judgment, each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned.
ESV.4: For as in one body we have many members,  and the members do not all have the same function,
ESV.5: so we, though many, are one body in Christ, and individually members one of another.
ESV.6: Having gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, let us use them: if prophecy, in proportion to our faith;
ESV.7: if service, in our serving; the one who teaches, in his teaching;
ESV.8: the one who exhorts, in his exhortation; the one who contributes, in generosity; the one who leads,  with zeal; the one who does acts of mercy, with cheerfulness.
ESV.9: Let love be genuine. Abhor what is evil; hold fast to what is good.
ESV.10: Love one another with brotherly affection. Outdo one another in showing honor.
ESV.11: Do not be slothful in zeal, be fervent in spirit,  serve the Lord.
ESV.12: Rejoice in hope, be patient in tribulation, be constant in prayer.
ESV.13: Contribute to the needs of the saints and seek to show hospitality.
ESV.14: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse them.
ESV.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice, weep with those who weep.
ESV.16: Live in harmony with one another. Do not be haughty, but associate with the lowly.  Never be wise in your own sight.
ESV.17: Repay no one evil for evil, but give thought to do what is honorable in the sight of all.
ESV.18: If possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all.
ESV.19: Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave it  to the wrath of God, for it is written, “Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord.”
ESV.20: To the contrary, “if your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him something to drink; for by so doing you will heap burning coals on his head.”
ESV.21: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

NASB.1: Therefore I urge you, brethren, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies a living and holy sacrifice, acceptable to God, [which is] your spiritual service of worship.
NASB.2: And do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what the will of God is, that which is good and acceptable and perfect.
NASB.3: For through the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think more highly of himself than he ought to think; but to think so as to have sound judgment, as God has allotted to each a measure of faith.
NASB.4: For just as we have many members in one body and all the members do not have the same function,
NASB.5: so we, who are many, are one body in Christ, and individually members one of another.
NASB.6: Since we have gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, [each of us is to exercise them accordingly]: if prophecy, according to the proportion of his faith;
NASB.7: if service, in his serving; or he who teaches, in his teaching;
NASB.8: or he who exhorts, in his exhortation; he who gives, with liberality; he who leads, with diligence; he who shows mercy, with cheerfulness.
NASB.9: [Let] love [be] without hypocrisy. Abhor what is evil; cling to what is good.
NASB.10: [Be] devoted to one another in brotherly love; give preference to one another in honor;
NASB.11: not lagging behind in diligence, fervent in spirit, serving the Lord;
NASB.12: rejoicing in hope, persevering in tribulation, devoted to prayer,
NASB.13: contributing to the needs of the saints, practicing hospitality.
NASB.14: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse.
NASB.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice, and weep with those who weep.
NASB.16: Be of the same mind toward one another; do not be haughty in mind, but associate with the lowly. Do not be wise in your own estimation.
NASB.17: Never pay back evil for evil to anyone. Respect what is right in the sight of all men.
NASB.18: If possible, so far as it depends on you, be at peace with all men.
NASB.19: Never take your own revenge, beloved, but leave room for the wrath [of God], for it is written, 'VENGEANCE IS MINE, I WILL REPAY,' says the Lord.
NASB.20: 'BUT IF YOUR ENEMY IS HUNGRY, FEED HIM, AND IF HE IS THIRSTY, GIVE HIM A DRINK; FOR IN SO DOING YOU WILL HEAP BURNING COALS ON HIS HEAD.'
NASB.21: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

NLT.1: And so, dear brothers and sisters, I plead with you to give your bodies to God because of all he has done for you. Let them be a living and holy sacrifice — the kind he will find acceptable. This is truly the way to worship him.
NLT.2: Don’t copy the behavior and customs of this world, but let God transform you into a new person by changing the way you think. Then you will learn to know God’s will for you, which is good and pleasing and perfect.
NLT.3: Because of the privilege and authority God has given me, I give each of you this warning: Don’t think you are better than you really are. Be honest in your evaluation of yourselves, measuring yourselves by the faith God has given us.
NLT.4: Just as our bodies have many parts and each part has a special function,
NLT.5: so it is with Christ’s body. We are many parts of one body, and we all belong to each other.
NLT.6: In his grace, God has given us different gifts for doing certain things well. So if God has given you the ability to prophesy, speak out with as much faith as God has given you.
NLT.7: If your gift is serving others, serve them well. If you are a teacher, teach well.
NLT.8: If your gift is to encourage others, be encouraging. If it is giving, give generously. If God has given you leadership ability, take the responsibility seriously. And if you have a gift for showing kindness to others, do it gladly.
NLT.9: Don’t just pretend to love others. Really love them. Hate what is wrong. Hold tightly to what is good.
NLT.10: Love each other with genuine affection, and take delight in honoring each other.
NLT.11: Never be lazy, but work hard and serve the Lord enthusiastically.
NLT.12: Rejoice in our confident hope. Be patient in trouble, and keep on praying.
NLT.13: When God’s people are in need, be ready to help them. Always be eager to practice hospitality.
NLT.14: Bless those who persecute you. Don’t curse them; pray that God will bless them.
NLT.15: Be happy with those who are happy, and weep with those who weep.
NLT.16: Live in harmony with each other. Don’t be too proud to enjoy the company of ordinary people. And don’t think you know it all!
NLT.17: Never pay back evil with more evil. Do things in such a way that everyone can see you are honorable.
NLT.18: Do all that you can to live in peace with everyone.
NLT.19:
Dear friends, never take revenge. Leave that to the righteous anger of God. For the Scriptures say, “I will take revenge;
I will pay them back,”
says the LORD.
NLT.20:
Instead, “If your enemies are hungry, feed them.
If they are thirsty, give them something to drink.
In doing this, you will heap
burning coals of shame on their heads.”
NLT.21: Don’t let evil conquer you, but conquer evil by doing good.

WEB.1: Therefore I urge you, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies a living sacrifice, holy, acceptable to God, which is your spiritual service.
WEB.2: Don’t be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may prove what is the good, well-pleasing, and perfect will of God.
WEB.3: For I say, through the grace that was given me, to every man who is among you, not to think of himself more highly than he ought to think; but to think reasonably, as God has apportioned to each person a measure of faith.
WEB.4: For even as we have many members in one body, and all the members don’t have the same function,
WEB.5: so we, who are many, are one body in Christ, and individually members one of another.
WEB.6: Having gifts differing according to the grace that was given to us, if prophecy, let us prophesy according to the proportion of our faith;
WEB.7: or service, let us give ourselves to service; or he who teaches, to his teaching;
WEB.8: or he who exhorts, to his exhorting: he who gives, let him do it with liberality; he who rules, with diligence; he who shows mercy, with cheerfulness.
WEB.9: Let love be without hypocrisy. Abhor that which is evil. Cling to that which is good.
WEB.10: In love of the brothers be tenderly affectionate to one another; in honor preferring one another;
WEB.11: not lagging in diligence; fervent in spirit; serving the Lord;
WEB.12: rejoicing in hope; enduring in troubles; continuing steadfastly in prayer;
WEB.13: contributing to the needs of the saints; given to hospitality.
WEB.14: Bless those who persecute you; bless, and don’t curse.
WEB.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice. Weep with those who weep.
WEB.16: Be of the same mind one toward another. Don’t set your mind on high things, but associate with the humble. Don’t be wise in your own conceits.
WEB.17: Repay no one evil for evil. Respect what is honorable in the sight of all men.
WEB.18: If it is possible, as much as it is up to you, be at peace with all men.
WEB.19: Don’t seek revenge yourselves, beloved, but give place to God’s wrath. For it is written, “Vengeance belongs to me; I will repay, says the Lord.”
WEB.20: Therefore “If your enemy is hungry, feed him. If he is thirsty, give him a drink; for in doing so, you will heap coals of fire on his head.”
WEB.21: Don’t be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

CSB17.1: Therefore, brothers and sisters, in view of the mercies of God, I urge you <sup>ⓑ</sup> to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, <sup>ⓒ</sup> holy and pleasing to God; this is your true worship. <sup>[1]</sup>
CSB17.2: Do not be conformed <sup>ⓓ</sup> to this age, <sup>ⓔ</sup> but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, <sup>ⓕ</sup> so that you may discern what is the good, pleasing, and perfect will <sup>ⓖ</sup> of God.
CSB17.3: For by the grace <sup>ⓗ</sup> given to me, I tell everyone among you not to think of himself more highly than he should think. <sup>ⓘ</sup> Instead, think sensibly, as God has distributed a measure of faith <sup>ⓙ</sup> to each one.
CSB17.4: Now as we have many parts in one body, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and all the parts do not have the same function,
CSB17.5: in the same way we who are many <sup>ⓛ</sup> are one body in Christ <sup>ⓜ</sup> and individually members of one another.
CSB17.6: According to the grace given to us, we have different gifts: <sup>ⓝ</sup> If prophecy, <sup>ⓐ</sup> use it according to the proportion of one’s <sup>[2]</sup> faith;
CSB17.7: if service, <sup>ⓑ</sup> use it in service; if teaching, <sup>ⓒ</sup> in teaching;
CSB17.8: if exhorting, <sup>ⓓ</sup> in exhortation; giving, with generosity; <sup>ⓔ</sup> leading, <sup>ⓕ</sup> with diligence; showing mercy, with cheerfulness. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.9: Let love be without hypocrisy. <sup>ⓗ</sup> Detest evil; <sup>ⓘ</sup> cling to what is good.
CSB17.10: Love one another deeply as brothers and sisters. <sup>ⓙ</sup> Outdo one another in showing honor. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.11: Do not lack diligence in zeal; be fervent in the Spirit; <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup> serve the Lord. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.12: Rejoice in hope; <sup>ⓝ</sup> be patient in affliction; <sup>ⓐ</sup> be persistent in prayer. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.13: Share with the saints in their needs; <sup>ⓒ</sup> pursue hospitality. <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.14: Bless those who persecute you; <sup>ⓔ</sup> bless and do not curse.
CSB17.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice; <sup>ⓕ</sup> weep with those who weep.
CSB17.16: Live in harmony with one another. <sup>ⓖ</sup> Do not be proud; <sup>ⓗ</sup> instead, associate with the humble. Do not be wise in your own estimation. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.17: Do not repay anyone evil for evil. <sup>ⓙ</sup> Give careful thought to do what is honorable <sup>ⓚ</sup> in everyone’s eyes.
CSB17.18: If possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.19: Friends, do not avenge yourselves; instead, leave room for God’s wrath, because it is written, Vengeance belongs to me; I will repay, [4] ⓜ says the Lord.
CSB17.20:
But If your enemy is hungry, feed him. ⓝ
If he is thirsty, give him something to drink.
For in so doing
you will be heaping fiery coals on his head. [5] ⓐ
CSB17.21: Do not be conquered by evil, but conquer evil with good.

AMP.1: Therefore I urge you, brothers and sisters, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies [dedicating all of yourselves, set apart] as a living sacrifice, holy and well-pleasing to God, which is your rational (logical, intelligent) act of worship.
AMP.2: And do not be conformed to this world [any longer with its superficial values and customs], but be transformed and progressively changed [as you mature spiritually] by the renewing of your mind [focusing on godly values and ethical attitudes], so that you may prove [for yourselves] what the will of God is, that which is good and acceptable and perfect [in His plan and purpose for you].
AMP.3: For by the grace [of God] given to me I say to everyone of you not to think more highly of himself [and of his importance and ability] than he ought to think; but to think so as to have sound judgment, as God has apportioned to each a degree of faith [and a purpose designed for service].
AMP.4: For just as in one [physical] body we have many parts, and these parts do not all have the same function or special use,
AMP.5: so we, who are many, are [nevertheless just] one body in Christ, and individually [we are] parts one of another [mutually dependent on each other].
AMP.6: Since we have gifts that differ according to the grace given to us, each of us is to use them accordingly: if [someone has the gift of] prophecy, [let him speak a new message from God to His people] in proportion to the faith possessed;
AMP.7: if service, in the act of serving; or he who teaches, in the act of teaching;
AMP.8: or he who encourages, in the act of encouragement; he who gives, with generosity; he who leads, with diligence; he who shows mercy [in caring for others], with cheerfulness.
AMP.9: Love is to be sincere and active [the real thing—without guile and hypocrisy]. Hate what is evil [detest all ungodliness, do not tolerate wickedness]; hold on tightly to what is good.
AMP.10: Be devoted to one another with [authentic] brotherly affection [as members of one family], give preference to one another in honor;
AMP.11: never lagging behind in diligence; aglow in the Spirit, enthusiastically serving the Lord;
AMP.12: constantly rejoicing in hope [because of our confidence in Christ], steadfast and patient in distress, devoted to prayer [continually seeking wisdom, guidance, and strength],
AMP.13: contributing to the needs of God's people, pursuing [the practice of] hospitality.
AMP.14: Bless those who persecute you [who cause you harm or hardship]; bless and do not curse [them].
AMP.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice [sharing others' joy], and weep with those who weep [sharing others' grief].
AMP.16: Live in harmony with one another; do not be haughty [conceited, self-important, exclusive], but associate with humble people [those with a realistic self-view]. Do not overestimate yourself.
AMP.17: Never repay anyone evil for evil. Take thought for what is right and gracious and proper in the sight of everyone.
AMP.18: If possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone.
AMP.19: Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave the way open for God's wrath [and His judicial righteousness]; for it is written [in Scripture], "Vengeance is Mine, I will repay," says the Lord.
AMP.20: <e>But if your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink; for by doing this you will heap</e><e>burning coals on his head</e>."
AMP.21: Do not be overcome and conquered by evil, but overcome evil with good.

MSG.1: So here's what I want you to do, God helping you: Take your everyday, ordinary life—your sleeping, eating, going-to-work, and walking-around life—and place it before God as an offering. Embracing what God does for you is the best thing you can do for him.
MSG.2: Don't become so well-adjusted to your culture that you fit into it without even thinking. Instead, fix your attention on God. You'll be changed from the inside out. Readily recognize what he wants from you, and quickly respond to it. Unlike the culture around you, always dragging you down to its level of immaturity, God brings the best out of you, develops well-formed maturity in you.
MSG.3: I'm speaking to you out of deep gratitude for all that God has given me, and especially as I have responsibilities in relation to you. Living then, as every one of you does, in pure grace, it's important that you not misinterpret yourselves as people who are bringing this goodness to God. No, God brings it all to you. The only accurate way to understand ourselves is by what God is and by what he does for us, not by what we are and what we do for him.
MSG.4: In this way we are like the various parts of a human body. Each part gets its meaning from the body as a whole, not the other way around.
MSG.5: The body we're talking about is Christ's body of chosen people. Each of us finds our meaning and function as a part of his body. But as a chopped-off finger or cut-off toe we wouldn't amount to much, would we? So since we find ourselves fashioned into all these excellently formed and marvelously functioning parts in Christ's body,
MSG.6: let's just go ahead and be what we were made to be, without enviously or pridefully comparing ourselves with each other, or trying to be something we aren't. If you preach, just preach God's Message, nothing else;
MSG.7: if you help, just help, don't take over; if you teach, stick to your teaching;
MSG.8: if you give encouraging guidance, be careful that you don't get bossy; if you're put in charge, don't manipulate; if you're called to give aid to people in distress, keep your eyes open and be quick to respond; if you work with the disadvantaged, don't let yourself get irritated with them or depressed by them. Keep a smile on your face.
MSG.9: Love from the center of who you are; don't fake it. Run for dear life from evil; hold on for dear life to good.
MSG.10: Be good friends who love deeply; practice playing second fiddle.
MSG.11: Don't burn out; keep yourselves fueled and aflame. Be alert servants of the Master,
MSG.12: cheerfully expectant. Don't quit in hard times; pray all the harder.
MSG.13: Help needy Christians; be inventive in hospitality.
MSG.14: Bless your enemies; no cursing under your breath.
MSG.15: Laugh with your happy friends when they're happy; share tears when they're down.
MSG.16: Get along with each other; don't be stuck-up. Make friends with nobodies; don't be the great somebody.
MSG.17: Don't hit back; discover beauty in everyone.
MSG.18: If you've got it in you, get along with everybody.
MSG.19: Don't insist on getting even; that's not for you to do. "I'll do the judging," says God. "I'll take care of it."
MSG.20: Our Scriptures tell us that if you see your enemy hungry, go buy that person lunch, or if he's thirsty, get him a drink. Your generosity will surprise him with goodness.
MSG.21: Don't let evil get the best of you; get the best of evil by doing good.

NIV.1:
Living Sacrifices
Therefore, I urge you, brothers, in view of God’s mercy, to offer your bodies as living sacrifices, holy and pleasing to God—this is your spiritual act of worship.
NIV.2: Do not conform any longer to the pattern of this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind. Then you will be able to test and approve what God’s will is—his good, pleasing and perfect will.
NIV.3: For by the grace given me I say to every one of you: Do not think of yourself more highly than you ought, but rather think of yourself with sober judgment, in accordance with the measure of faith God has given you.
NIV.4: Just as each of us has one body with many members, and these members do not all have the same function,
NIV.5: so in Christ we who are many form one body, and each member belongs to all the others.
NIV.6: We have different gifts, according to the grace given us. If a man’s gift is prophesying, let him use it in proportion to his faith.
NIV.7: If it is serving, let him serve; if it is teaching, let him teach;
NIV.8: if it is encouraging, let him encourage; if it is contributing to the needs of others, let him give generously; if it is leadership, let him govern diligently; if it is showing mercy, let him do it cheerfully.
NIV.9:
Love
Love must be sincere. Hate what is evil; cling to what is good.
NIV.10: Be devoted to one another in brotherly love. Honor one another above yourselves.
NIV.11: Never be lacking in zeal, but keep your spiritual fervor, serving the Lord.
NIV.12: Be joyful in hope, patient in affliction, faithful in prayer.
NIV.13: Share with God’s people who are in need. Practice hospitality.
NIV.14: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse.
NIV.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice; mourn with those who mourn.
NIV.16: Live in harmony with one another. Do not be proud, but be willing to associate with people of low position. Do not be conceited.
NIV.17: Do not repay anyone evil for evil. Be careful to do what is right in the eyes of everybody.
NIV.18: If it is possible, as far as it depends on you, live at peace with everyone.
NIV.19: Do not take revenge, my friends, but leave room for God’s wrath, for it is written: “It is mine to avenge; I will repay,” says the Lord.
NIV.20:
On the contrary:
“If your enemy is hungry, feed him;
if he is thirsty, give him something to drink.
In doing this, you will heap burning coals on his head.”
NIV.21: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

NRSVCE.1: I appeal to you therefore, brothers and sisters, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your spiritual worship.
NRSVCE.2: Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewing of your minds, so that you may discern what is the will of God — what is good and acceptable and perfect.
NRSVCE.3: For by the grace given to me I say to everyone among you not to think of yourself more highly than you ought to think, but to think with sober judgment, each according to the measure of faith that God has assigned.
NRSVCE.4: For as in one body we have many members, and not all the members have the same function,
NRSVCE.5: so we, who are many, are one body in Christ, and individually we are members one of another.
NRSVCE.6: We have gifts that differ according to the grace given to us: prophecy, in proportion to faith;
NRSVCE.7: ministry, in ministering; the teacher, in teaching;
NRSVCE.8: the exhorter, in exhortation; the giver, in generosity; the leader, in diligence; the compassionate, in cheerfulness.
NRSVCE.9: Let love be genuine; hate what is evil, hold fast to what is good;
NRSVCE.10: love one another with mutual affection; outdo one another in showing honor.
NRSVCE.11: Do not lag in zeal, be ardent in spirit, serve the Lord.
NRSVCE.12: Rejoice in hope, be patient in suffering, persevere in prayer.
NRSVCE.13: Contribute to the needs of the saints; extend hospitality to strangers.
NRSVCE.14: Bless those who persecute you; bless and do not curse them.
NRSVCE.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice, weep with those who weep.
NRSVCE.16: Live in harmony with one another; do not be haughty, but associate with the lowly; do not claim to be wiser than you are.
NRSVCE.17: Do not repay anyone evil for evil, but take thought for what is noble in the sight of all.
NRSVCE.18: If it is possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all.
NRSVCE.19: Beloved, never avenge yourselves, but leave room for the wrath of God; for it is written, “Vengeance is mine, I will repay, says the Lord.”
NRSVCE.20: No, “if your enemies are hungry, feed them; if they are thirsty, give them something to drink; for by doing this you will heap burning coals on their heads.”
NRSVCE.21: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

NET.1: Therefore I exhort you, brothers and sisters, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a sacrifice — alive, holy, and pleasing to God — which is your reasonable service.
NET.2: Do not be conformed to this present world, but be transformed by the renewing of your mind, so that you may test and approve what is the will of God — what is good and well-pleasing and perfect.
NET.3: For by the grace given to me I say to every one of you not to think more highly of yourself than you ought to think, but to think with sober discernment, as God has distributed to each of you a measure of faith.
NET.4: For just as in one body we have many members, and not all the members serve the same function,
NET.5: so we who are many are one body in Christ, and individually we are members who belong to one another.
NET.6: And we have different gifts according to the grace given to us. If the gift is prophecy, that individual must use it in proportion to his faith.
NET.7: If it is service, he must serve; if it is teaching, he must teach;
NET.8: if it is exhortation, he must exhort; if it is contributing, he must do so with sincerity; if it is leadership, he must do so with diligence; if it is showing mercy, he must do so with cheerfulness.
NET.9: Love must be without hypocrisy. Abhor what is evil, cling to what is good.
NET.10: Be devoted to one another with mutual love, showing eagerness in honoring one another.
NET.11: Do not lag in zeal, be enthusiastic in spirit, serve the Lord.
NET.12: Rejoice in hope, endure in suffering, persist in prayer.
NET.13: Contribute to the needs of the saints, pursue hospitality.
NET.14: Bless those who persecute you, bless and do not curse.
NET.15: Rejoice with those who rejoice, weep with those who weep.
NET.16: Live in harmony with one another; do not be haughty but associate with the lowly. Do not be conceited.
NET.17: Do not repay anyone evil for evil; consider what is good before all people.
NET.18: If possible, so far as it depends on you, live peaceably with all people.
NET.19: Do not avenge yourselves, dear friends, but give place to God’s wrath, for it is written, “Vengeance is mine, I will repay,” says the Lord.
NET.20: Rather, if your enemy is hungry, feed him; if he is thirsty, give him a drink; for in doing this you will be heaping burning coals on his head.
NET.21: Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.
